title: Did Anno Have a Grand Design Goal or Purpose to NGE
display_title: Did Anno Have a Grand Design Goal or Purpose to NGE
wikitext:
[]: Did Anno Have a Grand Design, Goal, or Purpose to NGE?             Quote Post by MisatoCrush » Mon Jan 20, 2014 9:50 am As I was diving deep into ancient threads I came across some quoted or paraphrased dialogue from one of the episodes. I believe it was referring to the Instrumentality Program and saying it's goal was to help mankind evolve and improve itself. Do you think Anno wanted his art, his creation to serve as a lesson to viewers and society in any way? Several things come to mind, but mainly: If you love someone, don't hold it in. Tell them! Life's too short and tragic. Don't neglect your children. Etc.

[]: Re: Did Anno Have a Grand Design, Goal, or Purpose to NGE?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Mon Jan 20, 2014 10:57 am I can only see to purposes / messages in NGE: 1. Criticizing Nerds / Otakus 2. Thematizing sociological constructivism (HIP, Shinji's social issues, etc.) It would surprise me, if there isn't already a thread about EVAs messages.

[]: Re:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 20, 2014 11:08 am MisatoCrush wrote: Did Anno Have a Grand Design, Goal, or Purpose to NGE?

[]:             Quote Post by MisatoCrush » Mon Jan 20, 2014 11:22 am Sweet thanks! Yeah I saw some Anno interview threads but thought it'd be ok to address this directly with a new thread. Cheers.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Jan 20, 2014 11:32 am Perhaps this is why Shinji is a bit more different in the movies than compared to the tv show to reflect Anno's change in personalty & how he sees himself now

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 20, 2014 12:22 pm That certainly has been suggested, and seems a reasonable presumption.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Jan 20, 2014 6:26 pm I think it's pretty much what Eva Monkey pointed to. Evangelion is his magnum opus and by far the work where he put the most of himself into. It's actually both overpowering and inspiring to see and feel all the passion and vital energy the staff at Gainax/Khara put in Eva and their other works.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Tue Jan 21, 2014 6:36 am Dream wrote: I think it's pretty much what Eva Monkey pointed to. Evangelion is his magnum opus and by far the work where he put the most of himself into. It's actually both overpowering and inspiring to see and feel all the passion and vital energy the staff at Gainax/Khara put in Eva and their other works.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Jan 21, 2014 11:49 am Well yeah, obviously he didn't perfectly visualize NGE from the get-go and Gainax always was a very particular studio in that everyone could add or suggest ideas or directions of some sorts. Many people from Gainax had a very important hand in how Evangelion turned out but ultimately it's all Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jan 21, 2014 12:18 pm Dataprime wrote: Perhaps this is why Shinji is a bit more different in the movies than compared to the tv show to reflect Anno's change in personalty & how he sees himself now

[]: Did Anno Have a Grand Design, Goal, or Purpose to NGE?             Quote Post by MisatoCrush » Mon Jan 20, 2014 9:50 am As I was diving deep into ancient threads I came across some quoted or paraphrased dialogue from one of the episodes. I believe it was referring to the Instrumentality Program and saying it's goal was to help mankind evolve and improve itself. Do you think Anno wanted his art, his creation to serve as a lesson to viewers and society in any way? Several things come to mind, but mainly: If you love someone, don't hold it in. Tell them! Life's too short and tragic. Don't neglect your children. Etc.

[]: Re: Did Anno Have a Grand Design, Goal, or Purpose to NGE?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Mon Jan 20, 2014 10:57 am I can only see to purposes / messages in NGE: 1. Criticizing Nerds / Otakus 2. Thematizing sociological constructivism (HIP, Shinji's social issues, etc.) It would surprise me, if there isn't already a thread about EVAs messages.
